source_jobs.title: Purchasing Agent, Los Angeles
source_jobs.description:
About Us:

Homebound is on a mission to make it possible for anyone, anywhere, to build a home using technology. Created by an experienced team of construction, real estate, design, and technology experts, Homebound is transforming the residential construction industry by improving the costly and inefficient process of building a home. 

We’ve created an entirely new way to build homes with technology powering every stage from start to finish to provide a seamless experience for our customers. Homeowners across the country can choose where they want to live, select a home plan that’s perfect for them, then personalize and buy it, all online. Homebound has raised $150M in capital from leading venture capitalists like Google, Khosla, Thrive Ventures, and we’re scaling quickly in places like Texas, Colorado and Florida. Come build your future with us.

.

About The Role:

As a member of the Purchasing Team, the Purchasing Agent will be responsible for executing Homebound's purchasing process in Los Angeles. The Purchasing Agent is responsible for executing POs, VPOs, completing budget validation exercises and providing design input and emphasis to the Architecture and Design department from a cost and supply chain point of view.

The ideal candidate has excellent numerical skills and an analytical mindset, and is eager to contribute to the development of a fast-paced, technology-driven construction company. The ideal candidate also has a well-rounded understanding of supply chains, trade and commodity negotiations, market trends, and trade relationship building.



What You’ll Do:

 - Drive comprehensive project documentation and facilitate communication among cross-functional stakeholders

 - Manage the PO process to ensure alignment with project specifications and budgets

 - Independently manage the budget lifecycle, including approvals, adjustments, variance analysis, and performance optimization strategies

 - Ensure agreements are structured to maximize company value and leverage knowledge of local construction costs

 - Lead trade partner performance reviews using Blueprint scorecards; identify improvement areas and build long-term partnerships

 - Attend home and prototype walks to understand project field adjustments, HVAC placements, and trade execution

 - Independently manage complex blueprint changes, update templates, and contribute to cross-functional product development

 - Ensure timely PO releases aligned with construction schedules

 - Enter budgets into Homebound’s platform to initiate new projects
   Contribute to the development of scopes of work, unit pricing, and supplier/trade contract documentation

 - Use Homebound’s proprietary technology to manage workflows and drive efficiency

What You’ll Bring:

 - 4+ years of experience in Purchasing or related roles within custom or production homebuilding

 - Degree in Construction Management, Building Sciences, or related field preferred

 - Strong proficiency in Google Sheets or Excel for data entry and tracking

 - Familiarity with MEP systems and their integration into residential projects

 - Working knowledge of local building codes and their application

 - Estimating experience using tools such as PlanSwift or On-Screen Takeoffs

 - Ability to read and interpret architectural and construction documents

 - Proven ability to collaborate across teams - especially Growth, Construction Operations, and Finance - to solve key business problems

 - Excellent analytical, problem-solving, organizational, and communication skills

 - High attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously



Our Commitment:

We are focused on building a diverse and inclusive workforce. If you’re excited about this role, but do not meet 100% of the qualifications listed above, we encourage you to apply. To apply, please submit an application with your resume on the Career’s page.

Homebound is an Equal Opportunity Employer and considers applicants for em

source_jobs.title: Architecture Associate, Greater Tampa
source_jobs.description:
About Us:

Homebound is on a mission to make it possible for anyone, anywhere, to build a home using technology. Created by an experienced team of construction, real estate, design, and technology experts, Homebound is transforming the residential construction industry by improving the costly and inefficient process of building a home. 

We’ve created an entirely new way to build homes with technology powering every stage from start to finish to provide a seamless experience for our customers. Homeowners across the country can choose where they want to live, select a home plan that’s perfect for them, then personalize and buy it, all online. Homebound has raised $150M in capital from leading venture capitalists like Google, Khosla, Thrive Ventures, and we’re scaling quickly in places like Texas, Colorado and Florida. Come build your future with us.


ARCHITECTURE ASSOCIATE

Architectural design and process management are central to how Homebound delivers high-quality homes efficiently across multiple markets. With architecture leading the early phases of each project, Homebound maintains a common thread through pre-construction services, coordinating design, construction documentation, and preparing projects for permitting and construction.

Homebound operates with a hybrid model where internal architects guide design direction and vision while a network of external consultants produces construction documentation. The Architecture Associate plays a central part in managing those consultant relationships, coordinating design development, and ensuring all architectural deliverables meet Homebound's quality and documentation standards. Experience aligned with a Project Architect or Architectural Project Manager is expected, with the ability to guide consultants, review design documentation, and keep projects moving efficiently from design through permitting.

Beyond project execution, this role will drive architectural plan development and the overall architectural process, contributing to the evolution of Homebound's plan series. The role supports exterior architectural character, materials, and design palettes to ensure homes feel distinctive, cohesive, and thoughtfully designed across markets. This role is well suited for someone who is a strong project manager with a designer’s mindset - someone who cares about proportion, materiality, and the details that elevate residential architecture.

This role will primarily support Florida markets including Tampa, St. Petersburg, and Jacksonville - candidates must be located within these markets.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Coordinate external architectural partners and consultants through pre-construction, managing communication, reviewing deliverables, and providing markups to ensure alignment with Homebound standards

 - Manage consultant selection, onboarding, and ongoing relationship management within Homebound's architectural partner network

 - Prepare and review construction documents prior to agency submittal and support the permitting process, including responding to jurisdictional comments and coordinating revisions

 - Ensure schematic design intent carries through design development and construction documentation, maintaining alignment with Homebound standards and project budgets

 - Support the development and refinement of Homebound's plan series, adapting layouts and architectural features to meet local zoning, site, and code requirements across markets

 - Provide design direction to external architects on exterior architecture, façade composition, and material selections, with a focus on strong residential design and cohesive architectural expression

 - Review and coordinate Revit models produced by external architects, ensuring compliance with Homebound modeling standards and documentation requirements

 - Partner with Product Development, Construction Operations, Procurement, and Sales teams to align architectural deliverables with construction and product strategy

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
About Us:

Homebound is on a mission to make it possible for anyone, anywhere, to build a home using technology. Created by an experienced team of construction, real estate, design, and technology experts, Homebound is transforming the residential construction industry by improving the costly and inefficient process of building a home. 

We’ve created an entirely new way to build homes with technology powering every stage from start to finish to provide a seamless experience for our customers. Homeowners across the country can choose where they want to live, select a home plan that’s perfect for them, then personalize and buy it, all online. Homebound has raised $150M in capital from leading venture capitalists like Google, Khosla, Thrive Ventures, and we’re scaling quickly in places like Texas, Colorado and Florida. Come build your future with us.



About The Role:

Homebound is seeking high energy and organized Sales Development Representatives to seek and engage individuals that are in the market to sell, rebuild, or buy a new home, through outbound prospecting methods including phone, email, LinkedIn and traditional outreach.



Whether prospects want to sell their property, need to rebuild their homes due to a natural disaster, or are looking to custom build a new home, SDRs will focus their energy on effectively communicating Homebound’s value props and building relationships with customers, with the ultimate goal of connecting them with one of our Land Acquisition Agents or Account Executives for a meeting. Ultimately, SDRs are responsible for boosting sales and contributing to Homebound’s rapid growth.



This role can be based out of Dallas - TX, Houston - TX, Tampa - FL, or Los Angeles, CA.




WHAT YOU’LL DO:

 - Contact potential clients through cold calls, emails, LinkedIn, and other methods

 - Schedule in-person meetings (or phone calls) for Land Acquisition Agents/Account Executives

 - Work closely with Land Acquisition Agents and Account Executives to ensure meeting hand-off successfully occurs

 - Act as the subject matter expert on homebuilding, providing prospects with peace of mind that Homebound is unlike any company in the industry

 - Qualify inbound leads from marketing campaigns, demo requests, emails/calls, etc.

 - Present Homebound’s value props in a clear and concise manner

 - Proactively identify potential prospects and find contact information using prospecting software

 - Diligently update Salesforce, ensuring our CRM system stays up-to-date with correct information

 - Have full knowledge of target prospect base and manage an ongoing prospect pipeline

 - Report to sales leadership on numbers including outreach, connected calls, meetings scheduled, etc.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR:

 - You have excellent verbal and written communication skills and enjoy interacting with people via phone, email and social media to articulate ideas and gain their trust, and have experience drafting clear communications that demonstrate attention to detail.

 - You have experience as an outbound prospector, including making cold calls, sending emails, and prospecting using tools like LinkedIn.

 - You have a track record of personal accountability, and a high standard of integrity and professionalism.

 - You’ve demonstrated resilience and a desire to succeed in the face of new and/or unforeseen challenges.

 - You are a team-player with a strong desire to help enforce and improve internal processes.

 - A desire to learn, grow and get in on the ground floor of a company that is changing how homeowners build homes

 - You are receptive to feedback, with the willingness to learn and embrace continuous improvement

 - Bachelor's Degree with 1-2+ years of experience in a sales-related role

 - Background in homebuilding, real estate or construction sales is a bonus!



Compensation and Benefits at Homebound:

At Homebound, we care for our teammates by supporting their physical, mental, and financial wellbeing thr

source_jobs.title: Senior Associate, Investment Strategy
source_jobs.description:
About Us:

Homebound is on a mission to make it possible for anyone, anywhere, to build a home using technology. Created by an experienced team of construction, real estate, design, and technology experts, Homebound is transforming the residential construction industry by improving the costly and inefficient process of building a home. 

We’ve created an entirely new way to build homes with technology powering every stage from start to finish to provide a seamless experience for our customers. Homeowners across the country can choose where they want to live, select a home plan that’s perfect for them, then personalize and buy it, all online. Homebound has raised $150M in capital from leading venture capitalists like Google, Khosla, Thrive Ventures, and we’re scaling quickly in places like Texas, Colorado and Florida. Come build your future with us.



About The Role:

We are seeking a Senior Associate to join Homebound’s Investment Strategy team, focused on driving the growth and execution of our infill spec development program (BOOL). This role is responsible for helping define and deliver Homebound’s 8+ quarter infill growth plan across existing and new markets. The position sits at the center of acquisitions, home product, and operations, and is accountable for translating growth targets into clear market-level plans and ensuring those plans are executed. This is not a pure analytics or research role. It is a strategy-to-execution role requiring strong project management, cross-functional coordination, and the ability to drive outcomes in ambiguous, fast-moving environments.



What You’ll Do:

Infill Growth Strategy & Planning:

 - Co-own the development and maintenance of Homebound’s 8+ quarter infill growth plan, including lot acquisition targets, market expansion priorities, and sequencing

 - Translate goals into market-by-market action plans across existing and new geographies

 - Identify key constraints to growth (pricing, home product, operational bottlenecks) and define clear initiatives to address them

 - Partner with leadership to refine and evolve the infill BOOL strategy over time



Market Execution & Performance Management:

 - Track performance against infill acquisition targets at the market and submarket level; identify gaps and implement corrective actions

 - Work closely with Acquisitions to improve lot sourcing outcomes, including market focus, pricing strategy, and broker engagement

 - Support in-quarter troubleshooting to resolve issues impacting volume, pricing, or conversion

 - Help define and adjust market-level strategies (neighborhood focus, product mix, price points) to improve performance

Cross-Functional Program Management:

 - Drive coordination across Acquisitions, Architecture, Preconstruction, Product, and Construction Operations to ensure markets are set up to scale

 - Own key workstreams required to launch and ramp new infill markets

 - Act as the connective tissue between teams to ensure alignment and execution against infill plan

Market Expansion (Selective):

 - Support evaluation and planning for new infill markets as part of the broader growth roadmap

 - Define initial market approach, including target neighborhoods, product strategy, and ramp plan

 - Ensure new markets are operationally and strategically positioned to meet acquisition targets

Who You Are:

 - You have 5+ years of experience in consulting, strategy & operations, or a highly analytical role with significant execution responsibility

 - You have experience owning complex, cross-functional projects and driving them from planning through execution

 - You are organized and can manage multiple workstreams while maintaining attention to detail, while also being comfortable operating in ambiguous environments and can quickly move from problem identification to action

 - You are effective at influencing cross-functional stakeholders without direct authority

 - You communicate clearly and concisely, both in wri

source_jobs.title: Marketing Associate
source_jobs.description:
About Us:

Homebound is on a mission to make it possible for anyone, anywhere, to build a home using technology. Created by an experienced team of construction, real estate, design, and technology experts, Homebound is transforming the residential construction industry by improving the costly and inefficient process of building a home. 

We’ve created an entirely new way to build homes with technology powering every stage from start to finish to provide a seamless experience for our customers. Homeowners across the country can choose where they want to live, select a home plan that’s perfect for them, then personalize and buy it, all online. Homebound has raised $150M in capital from leading venture capitalists like Google, Khosla, Thrive Ventures, and we’re scaling quickly in places like Texas, Colorado and Florida. Come build your future with us.



About The Role:

The Marketing Associate will support the execution of marketing initiatives across multiple markets and communities. This is a highly hands-on role focused on assisting with campaigns, coordinating deliverables, supporting events, updating marketing materials, and helping with day-to-day marketing operations across digital, print, signage, and sales support efforts. You will work closely with sales, construction, A&D, and external partners to bring campaigns and community launches to life, generate qualified demand, and support day-to-day marketing operations.

This is a hybrid role in one of our current markets: Dallas or Houston TX, Tampa or St. Petersburg FL or Los Angeles, CA.

What You’ll Do:

 - Assist with the execution of marketing campaigns across digital, print, email, events, and social media

 - Help coordinate timelines, deliverables, and marketing requests across multiple business lines

 - Support community launches, inventory marketing initiatives, and event preparation

 - Assist with website updates, landing page edits, and marketing content uploads

 - Coordinate sales collateral requests and marketing deliverables for internal teams

 - Work with the Graphic Designer to help organize and route creative requests

 - Assist with email marketing campaigns and lead nurturing initiatives

 - Help maintain brand consistency across marketing materials and communications

 - Support event logistics, vendor coordination, and promotional efforts

 - Assist with reporting, marketing organization, and administrative support as needed

 - Collaborate cross-functionally with sales, construction, and operations teams

What You’ll Bring:

 - Experience:
   
   - 4+ years of marketing experience with 2+ years of experience in the homebuilding or related real estate industry required
   
   - Experience in launching local communities required
   
   - Experience with top homebuilders and exposure to central strategy teams is preferred

Organization & Execution:
Highly organized with strong attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines in a fast-paced environment. Comfortable supporting marketing projects across digital, print, events, and sales initiatives while maintaining strong follow-through and communication.

Drive & Adaptability:
Eager to learn and grow within a fast-paced startup environment. Able to adapt to shifting priorities, take initiative when needed, and maintain a positive attitude while supporting a variety of marketing efforts.

Collaboration:
Strong team player with excellent communication and interpersonal skills. Comfortable collaborating across departments and working closely with internal teams, external partners, and vendors to support marketing initiatives and ensure projects move forward efficiently.

Quality & Attention to Detail:
Committed to producing thoughtful, high-quality work with strong attention to detail. Passionate about branding, customer experience, and supporting initiatives that help create an elevated homebuying experience.



Our Commitment:

We are focused on building a diverse and inclu

source_jobs.title: Technical Lead Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Us:

Homebound is on a mission to make it possible for anyone, anywhere, to build a home using technology. Created by an experienced team of construction, real estate, design, and technology experts, Homebound is transforming the residential construction industry by improving the costly and inefficient process of building a home. 

We’ve created an entirely new way to build homes with technology powering every stage from start to finish to provide a seamless experience for our customers. Homeowners across the country can choose where they want to live, select a home plan that’s perfect for them, then personalize and buy it, all online. Homebound has raised $150M in capital from leading venture capitalists like Google, Khosla, Thrive Ventures, and we’re scaling quickly in places like Texas, Colorado and Florida. Come build your future with us.




ABOUT THE ROLE:

We're hiring a Tech Lead Manager (Staff+) to lead a small team building the platform that is modernizing homebuilding. This is a leadership-track role for a senior engineer ready to grow their scope and impact. You'll stay hands-on as a technical leader while directly managing a team of 2–4 engineers.

The role blends deep technical ownership with people leadership. You'll set technical direction for your domain, ship production-grade code, and be accountable for the growth, output, and delivery of your team. The work spans the full stack and covers both internal tools that power construction operations and customer-facing experiences for homeowners and trades.

We're a remote-first team. You'll work from wherever you do your best work, with regular offsites to get together in person. You'll own entire product areas, make real technical decisions, and work directly with engineering leadership, product, and design to take features from concept to production.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

Technical Leadership:

 - Set technical direction for your domain. Contribute to architecture decisions, API contracts, and data models that shape how our systems scale and evolve.

 - Design, build, and deliver features across the platform. You own the full lifecycle from technical design through deployment and monitoring.

 - Stay hands-on. Write and deploy high-quality, production-grade code on a regular basis.

 - Raise the bar across engineering. Review designs, improve developer experience, and help other engineers work through complex problems.

People Leadership:

 - Directly manage a team of 2–4 engineers. Hold regular 1:1s, give clear and actionable feedback, and lead growth and performance conversations.

 - Own your team's delivery. Drive planning, prioritization, and execution, and be accountable for outcomes.

 - Coach and develop your engineers. Help them grow their skills, scope, and careers, and create the conditions for them to do their best work.

 - Partner with product and design to translate messy, real-world processes into clean, reliable software and to make tradeoffs that balance speed and quality.

 - Help grow the team. Recruit, interview, and raise the bar on who we hire.


ABOUT YOU:

 - 10+ years of professional software engineering experience, with a track record of shipping products that land real-world impact.

 - Experience as a tech lead, team lead, or engineering manager. You've led projects and/or people, whether formally or informally, and prior direct management experience is a strong plus.

 - Built and delivered full-stack features across modern web technologies. Our stack includes TypeScript, Node, React, GraphQL, PostgreSQL, and AWS. We care more about your ability to learn and build than checking every box.

 - Actively using AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, etc.) in your daily workflow. You can explain how they change the way you approach problems and how you'd bring that leverage to a team.

 - Demonstrated leadership and ownership. Mentoring engineers, influencing technical direction, and being the person people depend on.

 - Designed s

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant
source_jobs.description:
About Us:

Homebound is on a mission to make it possible for anyone, anywhere, to build a home using technology. Created by an experienced team of construction, real estate, design, and technology experts, Homebound is transforming the residential construction industry by improving the costly and inefficient process of building a home. 

We’ve created an entirely new way to build homes with technology powering every stage from start to finish to provide a seamless experience for our customers. Homeowners across the country can choose where they want to live, select a home plan that’s perfect for them, then personalize and buy it, all online. Homebound has raised $150M in capital from leading venture capitalists like Google, Khosla, Thrive Ventures, and we’re scaling quickly in places like Texas, Colorado and Florida. Come build your future with us.

.Role Overview:

As the Executive Assistant you will provide high-level support to VP+ leaders with complex calendars and responsibilities across the organization. This role requires a highly resourceful, proactive, and solutions-oriented professional who can anticipate needs, navigate ambiguity, and operate with a high degree of autonomy. Reporting to our Senior Executive Assistant, you will be a critical member of the team, ensuring that the leaders you support are maximally effective in our high-growth, fast-paced environment. The ideal candidate brings sound judgment, exceptional communication skills, excellent attention to detail, and a proactive mindset - comfortable owning complex projects and cross-functional coordination from end to end.

Must be located in the Dallas area and able to work from our Dallas office up to 3 days per week.




WHAT YOU'LL DO:

 - Provide comprehensive calendar management and administrative support for VP+ leaders with complex, fast-moving schedules - proactively flagging conflicts, anticipating priorities, and ensuring each day runs as smoothly as possible.

 - Serve as a trusted gatekeeper and strategic partner to the executives you support, exercising sound judgment to protect their time and independently prioritizing requests and communications.

 - Take full ownership of ensuring the leaders you support are always prepared, organized, and set up for success - proactively managing information flow and anticipating what they need before they ask.

 - Communicate effectively and independently across multiple departments including People Ops, Construction, Growth, Tech and others - facilitating cross-functional conversations and ensuring information is shared accurately and in a timely manner.

 - Independently plan and coordinate team events including offsites (virtual and in-person), and may lead significant corporate events and larger-scale company gatherings.

 - Take ownership of moderately complex projects, including meeting preparation, presentations, and ad hoc initiatives - driving them to completion with limited oversight and contributing to team objectives.

 - Independently arrange travel plans and coordinate logistics with all attendees, ensuring seamless execution.

 - Monitor and manage communications outside of standard hours to address time-sensitive needs from executives, independently prioritizing responses as appropriate.

 - Actively identify and evaluate process inefficiencies, independently implementing improvements and ensuring alignment with team and organizational objectives.

 - Use AI assistants and other technology to advance the role’s capabilities and output.

 - Assist with office management, conference room booking and other workplace projects as needed.

 - Assist with other ad-hoc administrative and people operations tasks and projects as assigned.




WHO YOU ARE:

 - 4+ years of experience as an executive assistant.

 - Bachelor’s degree, or equivalent combination of education and experience.

 - Experience in a startup environment supporting multiple VP+ leaders required.

 - Experience supporting C-suite l

source_jobs.title: Implementation Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Us:

Homebound is on a mission to make it possible for anyone, anywhere, to build a home using technology. Created by an experienced team of construction, real estate, design, and technology experts, Homebound is transforming the residential construction industry by improving the costly and inefficient process of building a home. 

We’ve created an entirely new way to build homes with technology powering every stage from start to finish to provide a seamless experience for our customers. Homeowners across the country can choose where they want to live, select a home plan that’s perfect for them, then personalize and buy it, all online. Homebound has raised $150M in capital from leading venture capitalists like Google, Khosla, Thrive Ventures, and we’re scaling quickly in places like Texas, Colorado and Florida. Come build your future with us.



About The Role:

As the Implementation Manager, you will ensure that Homebound's technology and AI deliver measurable improvements in speed, quality, and cost across our homebuilding operations. You will lead technology implementation, change management, process optimization, and cross-functional execution, helping bridge the gap between our product and operations teams to drive platform adoption and operational excellence.

This is a highly visible, hands-on role for a self-starter who thrives in ambiguity, influences without authority, and operates as both a strategic thinker and strong executor. Reporting to technology leadership, you will partner closely with product, operations, and field teams to help scale the systems, tools, and processes transforming a $1 Trillion industry.



What You'll Do:

 - End-to-End Technology Implementation: Develop launch strategy for proprietary technology across field and central operations. Own rollout timelines, stakeholder alignment, and training programs to ensure smooth deployments.

 - Change Management & Field Enablement: Drive change management initiatives. Establish shared goals, document best practices, and translate field feedback into product insights in partnership with product managers. Design and lead training programs with clear learning objectives and measurement goals. Serve as an escalation point for operators, without becoming a critical path.

 - Platform Adoption and Impact: Own and track KPIs related to platform adoption, engagement, and operational performance. Support efforts to ensure technology is generating measurable business results and reliable data to train our AI models. Diagnose root cause of impact blockers and drive interventions to course correct.

 - Workflow and Process Optimization: Partner with operations to design and implement optimized workflows within the platform. Identify bottlenecks and recommend improvements to execution speed, quality, and consistency. Synthesize insights and observed needs into actionable input for the product team.

 - Cross-Functional Program Delivery: Contribute to complex, cross-functional initiatives that span end-to-end operations. Build strong working relationships across teams and maintain effective collaboration with front line construction partners.

What You'll Bring:

 - 5+ years of experience in technical program coordination, solutions implementation, operations, project management, or process improvement.

 - Demonstrated ability to support change management and technology adoption in operationally complex or distributed environments.

 - Experience in residential construction, homebuilding, field operations technology, or similarly hands-on industries is a strong plus.

 - Willingness to travel regularly, with field immersion in Texas expected at least monthly.

 - Strong analytical skills including KPI tracking, root cause analysis, and data-informed decision making.

 - Excellent communication skills with the ability to translate between field needs, technical teams, and business stakeholders.

 - A structured thinker who can balance tactical execution with

source_jobs.title: Superintendent, Dallas
source_jobs.description:
About Us:

Homebound is on a mission to make it possible for anyone, anywhere, to build a home using technology. Created by an experienced team of construction, real estate, design, and technology experts, Homebound is transforming the residential construction industry by improving the costly and inefficient process of building a home. 

We’ve created an entirely new way to build homes with technology powering every stage from start to finish to provide a seamless experience for our customers. Homeowners across the country can choose where they want to live, select a home plan that’s perfect for them, then personalize and buy it, all online. Homebound has raised $150M in capital from leading venture capitalists like Google, Khosla, Thrive Ventures, and we’re scaling quickly in places like Texas, Colorado and Florida. Come build your future with us.

Role Overview

Homebound is seeking an experienced Construction Superintendent to join our Dallas team and lead field operations for our single-family home projects. As a Superintendent, you’ll be responsible for managing the entire construction process - from mobilization to delivery - with an uncompromising focus on safety and quality. Delivering homes on schedule and on budget is important, but secondary to building exceptional homes, safely.

Candidates must be based in Dallas, Texas area to be considered for this role.

What You'll Do

 - Oversee and coordinate all aspects of on-site construction for your assigned homes.

 - Manage the simultaneous construction of up to 15 single-family homes in various stages.
   Monitor workmanship and adherence to scopes of work through checklists and field inspections.

 - Execute daily operations using Homebound’s proprietary project management software.

 - Approve trade partner work and associated invoices based on quality and completeness.

 - Lead on-site implementation of safety protocols and ensure compliance with local/national codes and permitting authority requirements.

 - Maintain both digital and physical project documentation.
   Proactively initiate and communicate changes to scope, plans, and specifications.

 - Follow established operational procedures for communication, change orders, and financial tracking.

 - Keep daily logs of site activity and coordinate inspections to maintain schedule momentum.
   Collaborate closely with Project Managers to support buyouts and provide feedback on budgeting and trade performance.

 - Participate in client walks and obtain occupancy permits upon completion.

 - Perform other construction-related duties as needed.

What You'll Bring

 - 4+ years of hands-on experience as a Superintendent in residential home construction or relevant experience

 - Proficiency with schedule management tools (e.g., MS Project, BuilderTrend, CoConstruct, Procore).

 - Basic computer skills, including Google Suite.

 - A valid driver’s license





Our Commitment:

We are focused on building a diverse and inclusive workforce. If you’re excited about this role, but do not meet 100% of the qualifications listed above, we encourage you to apply. To apply, please submit an application with your resume on the Career’s page.

Homebound is an Equal Opportunity Employer and considers applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, orientation, national origin, age, disability, genetics or any other basis forbidden under federal, state, or local law. Homebound considers all qualified applicants in accordance with the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance.



Our Compensation Philosophy:

Our salary ranges are determined by role, level, and location. Please note that the salary range displayed on each job posting may vary by state. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.
